hero.title: Downcity
hero.headline: Scalable Agent Harness & Agentic Product Infrastructure for Builders.
hero.description: One product is one City, where agents live and work. Every City is powered by shared models and services from the Federation.
hero.coverLabel: An Agent ecosystem where residents live inside distinct product Cities that share capabilities through Federation.

ecosystem.caption: Agents live in product Cities connected through the same Federation
ecosystem.federationLabel: Federation provides shared capabilities to multiple product Cities

build.name: Build City
build.activity: Agents create, review, and ship together
build.label: Build City, where agents create, review, and ship together

research.name: Research City
research.activity: Agents explore, analyze, and share knowledge
research.label: Research City, where agents explore, analyze, and share knowledge

operations.name: Operations City
operations.activity: Agents monitor, support, and coordinate work
operations.label: Operations City, where agents monitor, support, and coordinate work

features.title: Infrastructure for real
features.description: Everything you keep rebuilding for every new AI product, organized into one reusable runtime layer.

repo.title: Repo-native boundary
repo.description: Every agent runs inside a real repository or folder. Skills are versioned, management config lives in one global DB, and runtime data stays in .downcity/.

runtime.title: Reusable runtime layer
runtime.description: Models, tools, tasks, memory, and services are configured once and reused across CLI, SDK, Console, and browser surfaces.

plugins.title: Plugin-native capabilities
plugins.description: Chat, Task, Memory, Shell, Web, Skill, Schedule, and Contact are all unified plugins that extend the agent without forking the core.

city.title: City-shared backend
city.description: Federation centralizes models, services, auth, and usage. Multiple Cities define product boundaries with distinct city IDs while their agents share the same runtime capabilities.

ops.title: Operable agents
ops.description: Run agents as managed daemons. Inspect status, review history, schedule tasks, and approve sensitive actions through CLI or Console.

surface.title: Multi-surface deployment
surface.description: Embed the same runtime into local CLI workflows, Node servers, Edge runtimes, or host applications with the Agent SDK and UI SDK.

architecture.title: Creators build Cities. Users run them. Federation powers them.
architecture.description: A creator sets up shared services in Federation and ships a product as a City with its agents. The user runs that City locally and connects it back to Federation when shared capabilities are needed.
architecture.diagramLabel: A creator sets up Federation and offers a City with agents to a user. The user runs the City on their computer and connects it to Federation for shared services.
architecture.creator: Creator
architecture.creatorLabel: Creator who sets up Federation and offers a product City with agents
architecture.userEnvironmentLabel: The user's computer contains a product City and its agents
architecture.federationLabel: Federation provides shared models, services, authentication, and usage
architecture.mobileCaption: One product is one City. Its agents run with the user and connect to Federation.

philosophy.title: Runtime, not framework.
philosophy.description: Downcity does not replace your stack. It gives agents a stable place to run, remember, and act—while products, tools, and humans stay in control.

runtime.title: Runtime as the reusable layer
runtime.description: Stop rebuilding model routing, auth, usage, and billing for every product. Configure them once in City and ship many surfaces.

repo.title: Repo as the execution boundary
repo.description: Agent behavior is grounded in the repository. Prompts, permissions, and artifacts travel with the code, not a distant platform.

city.title: City pays for the boring backend
city.description: Let City own accounts, balance, payment, model catalogs, and service routing so your product can focus on user experience.

useCases.title: One runtime. Many surfaces.
useCases.description: The same Downcity backend can power very different product experiences.

cli.title: CLI productivity tool
cli.description: A repo-local agent that summarizes code, runs tests, and manages tasks from the terminal.

browser.title: Browser extension agent
browser.description: An agent that reads the current page, takes notes, and acts on behalf of the user through web tools.

sdk.title: Embedded SDK workflow
sdk.description: Drop the Agent SDK into an existing app to add long-running sessions, memory, and plugin-driven actions.

backend.title: Multi-tenant product backend
backend.description: Use City to share auth, usage, and billing across customer-facing agents deployed on Node or Edge.

cta.title: Start building with Downcity
cta.description: Install the CLI, create your first agent project, and connect to City in minutes.

footer.tagline: Agent infrastructure for AI builders shipping many agent-powered products and workflows.
